This can be a unique alternative to holding Ireland under a march, however a personal union can be randomly integrated based on thediplomatic reputation of the player. If the player wishes to keep a permanent vassal, they may wish to simply vassalise the Irish and declare them a march. ##### Subjugating Scotland[edit|edit source] It would be recommended for the player to not declare Scotland as arival, as it will hinder their attempts to turn Scotland into a loyal subject. Conquering Scotland can be tricky due to them either beingguaranteed by or becomingallies with {[flag|France}}. You do not need to fight the French immediately however, as waiting may lead to the French being unwilling to join, breaking the alliance, or being able to directly combat the french. There are multiple ways that the French can be dealt with or ignored. Ignoring France is the first strategy, which is where the player ignores the French lands and occupies Scotland, waiting for the Scottish to capitulate whilst preventing French reinforcements with the English navy, which is larger than the French one at the start of the game. Another strategy is to remove France from the war by ceding land to them. If the player wishes to play a more isolationist England which does not really interfere with European affairs, it may be recommended to cede the lands in France which the French hold a core, both to remove them as a threat from England but also so that they will most likely not return until the French wish to control Calais (The only English province that France does not hold a core on at game start). The

in France which the French hold a core, both to remove them as a threat from England but also so that they will most likely not return until the French wish to control Calais (The only English province that France does not hold a core on at game start). The final strategy is to build up the army and navy as outlined in the section for forming the Angevin Kingdom, as whilst the goal of the player may not be to deal with France directly, it serves as a strategy to remove them from the conflict somewhat effectively. When subjugating Scotland, it may be advisable to also remove the Scottish core from theprovince of Mann, which can aid with increasing the opinion of Scotland and preventing events which can cause friction between Scotland and England relating to returning the core province. The player will not be able to annex The Isles if they choose to subjugate Scotland. Furthermore, reducingliberty desire of the Scottish can be achieved by destroying their army and navy before concluding the war, as well as repaying any debts that they might have through the subject interactions menu. Following the subjugation of Scotland, The Isles may then be conquered (They may have already been integrated by the Scottish, which may be relatively simple as they are a weak nation who are unlikely to gain any strong allies. Scotland also holds cores on their provinces, and so ceding those provinces to Scotland can aid in both reducing their liberty desire and reducing the burden on the player’sadministrative power, which can be precious in the early game due to England starting with a 0,0,0ruler. Once this has been completed, the English may wish to further conquer the Atlantic islands if they wish to pursue colonisation of the New World.

Once this has been completed, the English may wish to further conquer the Atlantic islands if they wish to pursue colonisation of the New World. ##### Passing the Act of Union of Scotland[edit|edit source] The Act of UnionParliamentary Debate holds similar requirements to the debate regarding France to form the Angevin Kingdom, requiringAdministrative Technology Level 10, however this Act formsGreat Britain, depending on the outcome of theDestiny of England mission. Scotland must hold less than 15 provinces however, when compared to France who must have less than 40. Once this issue has been passed, the nation of Great Britain will be formed. #### Forming the Angevin Kingdom[edit|edit source] Forming theAngevin Kingdomcan be difficult for players who are less experienced with the game, however, once the nation is formed it has the potential to become extremely powerful, dominating Western Europe with claims on the regions of France, Iberia, Italy and the Southern half of the Low Countries (As of 1.37.5). Once the Angevin Kingdom has formed, the player is given the option to releaseSpain,ItalyandIreland(provided they do not already exist) aspersonal union states through theParliament debatesdebates for the three nations separately. This is alongside the subjugation ofScotland. The Angevin Kingdom requires that the player reachesAdministrative Technologylevel 10 before forming the nation, generally meaning that the player will reach the mid 1500s before forming the nation unless he switch out of the English Parliament reform thus bypassing any technological requirements and French province limit. ##### Idea Groups[edit|edit source] Potential idea groups that may be suggested for the Angevin Kingdom's formation may be those of influence, which will aid in reducing the liberty desire of the French crown as

switch out of the English Parliament reform thus bypassing any technological requirements and French province limit. ##### Idea Groups[edit|edit source] Potential idea groups that may be suggested for the Angevin Kingdom's formation may be those of influence, which will aid in reducing the liberty desire of the French crown as well as increasing diplomatic reputation as is required by the Act of Union. Other suggestions for the idea groups when forming the Angevin Kingdom are those of Offensive and Quality in order to increase army quality and to further aid with consolidating France and further progression as the Angevin Kingdom, providingdiscipline,siege ability andleader bonuses. Once the Angevin Kingdom has been formed, it may be advisable to take Administrative ideas due to the high cost of controlling the land within France, which can number up to500 development by the time of integration as well as the lands within the British Isles. ##### Securing the French Union[edit|edit source] The early game presents two options when the Surrender of Maine event is triggered. The first option is to fightFranceand their allies for the subjugation of France using the casus belli from the Hundred Years War, and the second option is to return Maine to the French in order to prepare for a later French war to subjugate them (The casus belli is obtained from the English eventDestiny of England). The first option may not be suited for newer players due to the need to balance the Hundred Years War and theWars of the Roses Disaster, which will most likely fire at the same time. The second option can allow for the player to prepare for the war more in comparison to fighting the war against the French within the first year of the game start (Mean time to happen for theSurrender of Maineevent

Roses Disaster, which will most likely fire at the same time. The second option can allow for the player to prepare for the war more in comparison to fighting the war against the French within the first year of the game start (Mean time to happen for theSurrender of Maineevent is 12 months). Starting at the 11th of November 1444, the player may be advised to commence with an army and navy buildup to prepare for the inevitable French confrontation, for which it may be recommended to build enough transports to move entire armies from England to the European continent for the later war. Furthermore, it is recommended for the player to find strongallies who have also declared the French their rival (This may beAustria,Hungary,Castile,PortugalorBurgundy). These allies will not be useful in the case of the Hundred Years War CB, as the English are the aggressor and so allies cannot be called into the war. The Hundred Years War casus belli was updated in 1.36, where England must control the capital of France (normally Paris), and France must control the province of Maine. However, the French allies are automatically called into the war due to it being a defensive war. These can include Scotland,Switzerland,Savoy,Provence, orAragon(although there may be numerous other nations that the French may ally with). In the case that the player wishes to subjugate France in the Hundred Years War, then it is recommended that the player first attack Scotland (if they are allied to the French) due to the threat that they present to the English homeland, which can easily ruin a war if left unchecked. They may then be removed from the war with either land, ducats, war reparations, or any other terms which may help with the present war. Following this focus, the French may

to the threat that they present to the English homeland, which can easily ruin a war if left unchecked. They may then be removed from the war with either land, ducats, war reparations, or any other terms which may help with the present war. Following this focus, the French may have occupied the entirety of English France, or almost the enterity depending on the circumstances. As a result, it may be advised that the player request fleet basing rights and military access from a neighbouring nation to France, which could be a number of nations, as well as acquiring mercenaries which match or fall underneath the number of transports which the player owns or is building. The French army alongside their vassals regularly numbers between35,000 to 45,000 soldiers, meaning that these mercenaries may be necessary in order to finish the war. In this case, it may be advised to approach from either Castile or Burgundy, given that they regularly are also rivals of the French and so may offer these accesses freely. In the case that France has occupied the entirety of English France, the player may face their entire army being defeated if they are to suffer a defeat, meaning this strategy can be somewhat precarious. Following the landing, liberating the lands of England may be advisable in order to regain the tax and production income from the provinces, which can aid with preventing continuous loans. Following this, the removal of the French allies may further reduce the willingness of the French and their vassals to continue the war. The fort in Chartres may cause some issues with securing the war goal, as well as other forts such as Auvergne. of the French and their vassals to continue the war. The fort in Chartres may cause some issues with securing the war goal, as well as other forts such as Auvergne. The second strategy takes more time, which may lead to the French strengthening their position on the continent and so potentially a more difficult war if it was to be fought by solely the player, however the extra time that is offered by the game will allow the player to address theWars of the Roses disaster before the war to force the union on the French. This can be advantageous as a change of ruler can result in the personal union breaking before the player has been able to secure positive relations with the French. This strategy relies upon the allies that the player can gather and who they can call into the war against the French. Generally, the Iberians (Castile, Aragon and Portugal) can be called into the war with favours, as well as potentially the Austrians. These allies can essentially be used as shields who can help in their manpower pools and troop numbers against the French whilst Scotland is removed from the war as stated in the above section. Generally, advancing from the south with allies such as Castile or Portugal can avoid the player being trapped by the French and being destroyed by them, allowing for them to sweep north against the French and secure the personal union against the French. When securing the French lands, theunion must first be enforced (which uses60% warscore), after which it may be advisable to retake the cores of Armagnac and Maine in order to weaken the French further, which reduces their force limit and taxation ability and to benefit your own. Furthermore, it may be advisable to demand for money or war reparations in order to further reduce the income of France, which will help to secure the throne. This will also complete the mission Seize France's Throne. This mission grantsprestige, as well as+20% manpower recovery speed and-10% liberty desire, alongside permanent claims on the remainder of the France region. Following the union, it is critical that the player begins to improve relations with their new French subject in order to secure the union. The liberty desire of their new subject will not be a worry for a time due to the truce now in place with the French. As a result, they will be unable to have other nations support independance. In order to prevent the nation breaking away on the death of the Monarch however, the player must improve relations until they reach0 or above. As the relations begin to improve, it may be advisable to reduce the French liberty desire by supporting loyalists in France, further reducing theirliberty desire by 20%. ##### Dealing with the Dukes of France[edit|edit source] There are four missions which must be completed in order for the Angevin Kingdom to be formed following the subjugation of France, which are those of Secure the Duke's Crowns, Shatter French Nobility, Seize Burgundy, and finally The Angevin Kingdom. The dukes of France are those of Burgundy, Brittany and Provence. The mission Seize France's Throne grants claims on the provinces of Burgundy, Brittany and Provence within the region of France. Generally, Burgundy should be dealt

Secure the Duke's Crowns, Shatter French Nobility, Seize Burgundy, and finally The Angevin Kingdom. The dukes of France are those of Burgundy, Brittany and Provence. The mission Seize France's Throne grants claims on the provinces of Burgundy, Brittany and Provence within the region of France. Generally, Burgundy should be dealt with last due to their strength as a result of their own subjects making them tricky to deal with alongside a rebellious France. A consideration for the player to take into account is that theAct of Union Parliament Issuerequires that France holds less than 40provinces, therefore expanding France in order to reduce their liberty desire can be achieved to an extent. Of the other two missions that can be completed, Shatter French Nobility can be prepared for through the triggering of the Parliament issue when it is available, which causes the loss of one stability and the spawning of Pretender Rebels in the capital area of France. This issue also causes-20% nobility influence,+60 Yearly Tax Income and+15% Manpower Recovery Speed in order to help with dealing with the rebels. The player can deal with these somewhat quickly following the war, and this mission can be swiftly completed once France'sliberty desire is reduced below 30% The second of the two missions is that of Secure the Duke's Crowns. This mission requires that Provence and Brittany either are a subject of England or that they do not hold any land within the France region. The conquest of Brittany can be somewhat complex depending on the diplomatic situation of the country, who may ally nations rivalled to France as a result of them being threatened by them. This could lead to either a swift conquest of the country, or some time to restore the treasury of england and regain some manpower before conquering

depending on the diplomatic situation of the country, who may ally nations rivalled to France as a result of them being threatened by them. This could lead to either a swift conquest of the country, or some time to restore the treasury of england and regain some manpower before conquering the country. The other nation of Provence possesses the personal union subject ofLorraine, which can cause complications when attempting to secure the France region. Whilst they rarely manage to acquire many strong allies, they may still present a minor challenge, especially with a rebellious France. The nation of Lorraine causes complications due to the Act of Union requiring the conquest of the entire France region, and if they are left free of Provence, can cause complications due to them being a member of theHoly Roman Empire. Therefore, it can be recommended to annex Lorraine in the initial war with Provence in order to prevent this complication. This will take more than one war to fully annex Provence, however the first war should leave them sufficiently in debt to allow for an easy second conquest. This mission will aid in the subjugation of France through it granting+50 opinion and+15 trust. The final mission is that of Seize Burgundy. Burgundy is a stronger nation than that of the other French Dukes, possessing four subjects inNevers,Brabant,HollandandFlanders, alongside the ability to gather a number of allies due to their relative strength and development. As a result, this may lead to multiple wars and multiple conquests of the areas needed. There is a possibility of theBurgundian Inheritanceoccuring, provided that the English can remove the possibility of Burgundy declaring them as a rival and being able to secure aroyal marriage. However, this is still left to random chance as to whether or not it could be

conquests of the areas needed. There is a possibility of theBurgundian Inheritanceoccuring, provided that the English can remove the possibility of Burgundy declaring them as a rival and being able to secure aroyal marriage. However, this is still left to random chance as to whether or not it could be secured in this way. Furthermore, it is not necessary to annex any subjects (besides Nevers) of burgundy, meaning that all warscore can be focused on their lands within the French region, being those of Picardie, West Burgundy and East Burgundy, which can be taken in any order. Upon completing this mission, all three of these areas will lose 10 years ofseparatism, as well ascore creation cost being reduced by 25% and a further loss of 10 years ofSeparatism. ##### Passing the Act of Union Parliamentary Debate[edit|edit source] Following the completion of these missions, the player must own the France region either themselves or by a subject, the French must have at least150 opinion of the player, and the player must also have+2 diplomatic reputation. The conquest of France from this point consists of simply conquering the province of Avignon fromThe Papal State. This conquest can either be simple or complex depending on the alliance situation of both the player and the Papal State, however, the province must be acquired in some way before the Act of Union can be passed. The missionThe Angevin Kingdomwill result in the province of Anjou gaining 3 of eachdevelopment type, as well as local modifiers. The mission then unlocks the Act of Union, as well as granting claims on the North of Italy, the Aragonese lands in Iberia (besides the Balearic Islands) as well as the South of the Low Countries and Alsace. The Act of Union requires that England holds a number of provinces

The mission then unlocks the Act of Union, as well as granting claims on the North of Italy, the Aragonese lands in Iberia (besides the Balearic Islands) as well as the South of the Low Countries and Alsace. The Act of Union requires that England holds a number of provinces on both the English homeland (not including Scotland or Ireland) alongside some French provinces. Once the Act is passed through the Parliament system, the Angevin Kingdom will be formed. #### Colonial Britain[edit|edit source] Colonial England generally is a more defensive choice as it doesn't require direct conflict with continental Europe until later into the game. In this variation securing a strong economic base is vital to rapid colonization. One method is to conquer Scotland using the previously mentioned strategy of declaring war on one of the Irish minors allied with Scotland and including Scotland into the war as a co-belligerent. This will prevent a struggle with France. ##### Increasing early colonial range[edit|edit source] The challenge with an England centered colonization campaign is that they struggle to reach colonial regions until later technologies. A helpful way to increase England'scolonial range is to look toward neighboringNorwayand its Icelandic provinces and hiring a colonial advisor. Norway has a few neighboring provinces to the British isles. England can get involved in a war with their suzerain,Denmark, by supporting the independence ofSweden. An advantage to pursuing colonization through a northern strategy is that it confines England to the Northern portion of the Americas, away from the likely Iberian colonies in South America. A second way to increase colonial range is through conquest of parts of the Iberian Peninsula. Early fabrication of claims and rivaling of Castile can enable the release of potential vassals likeLeón(+1colonist as level 4 national idea) and securing the provinces of Galicia

the likely Iberian colonies in South America. A second way to increase colonial range is through conquest of parts of the Iberian Peninsula. Early fabrication of claims and rivaling of Castile can enable the release of potential vassals likeLeón(+1colonist as level 4 national idea) and securing the provinces of Galicia and The Canaries to put England closer to the colonizable regions of Africa and South America. Notably this method puts England in direct conflict for colonies with allyPortugaland now rivalCastile. Recommended idea groups for this direction areExplorationandExpansion.

This is acompilationand strategy article forEthiopia.Mechanics and flavor content aretranscludedfrom other pages. Traditions: Solomonid Claim Negusa Negast Hostile Borders Diplomatic Demeanor The Ark of the Covenant Expansionist Policy Reformed Armies Ambition: Ethiopiais a medium-sized country in eastern Africa, just north of theHorn of Africa. In 1444, it shares a border with itsvassals,DamotandHadiya, and withAlodia,Beja,Medri Bahri,Adal, andJanjiro. Starting with anEmpire-rank governmentand control of aCopticholy site, Ethiopia has the potential to be not just a contender for African superiority but to challenge for control of the Arabian peninsula and the Middle East.

### Ethiopian Government[edit|edit source] The Ethiopian government starts with the unique Tier 1 reformNegusa Nagast Monarchy. It gives the following benefits: - −75%Move capital cost modifier - −1National unrest - −10%Stability cost modifier - Has itinerantcapital:Every province in our capital's state gets the "Itinerant capital area" modifier, granting:−2Local unrest−15Minimum local autonomy−0.25Monthly autonomy change−10Years of separatism−0.3Monthly devastation+0.03Local prosperity growthAfter relocating our capital, we gain the"Capital reorganization"modifier for 5 years, granting:+1000%Move capital cost modifier - Every province in our capital's state gets the "Itinerant capital area" modifier, granting:−2Local unrest−15Minimum local autonomy−0.25Monthly autonomy change−10Years of separatism−0.3Monthly devastation+0.03Local prosperity growth - −2Local unrest - −15Minimum local autonomy - −0.25Monthly autonomy change - −10Years of separatism - −0.3Monthly devastation - +0.03Local prosperity growth - After relocating our capital, we gain the"Capital reorganization"modifier for 5 years, granting:+1000%Move capital cost modifier - +1000%Move capital cost modifier - Can recruitCawaregiments It triggers the eventThe Itinerant Capital of Ethiopiawhich provides a monthly autonomy change of+0.20for the rest of the game called Decentralized State. To balance this out the area the capital is located receives the following benefits: - −2Local Unrest - −15Minimum Local Autonomy - −0.25Month Autonomy Change - −10Years of separatism - −0.3Monthly Devastation - +0.03Local Prosperity Growth Moving the capital provides the country modifier of Capital Reorganization of+1000%Move Capital Cost for 5 years. Completing the missionCentralize the Statewill allow you to unlock theSolomonic Empirereform which can replace the Negusa Nagast Monarchy reform.

### Missions[edit|edit source] After theSonghai 1.32 patch, the new mission chains for Ethiopia are primarily focused around the liberation of thecoptsin the Middle East, the legacy of the Empire ofAksumand the contact with the Europeans.
### Events[edit|edit source] Ethiopia's events focus on imperial traditions. There are also two event chains regarding relations withPortugaland theMamluks.

### Reformation[edit|edit source] #### Restore Solomonid's Legacy The empire of Ethiopia is the spiritual successor of the Aksumite Empire, the home of the son of the legendary king Solomon and the queen of Sheba and the defender of the Coptic faith. Their fall has been a tragedy for the Ethiopian Coptic faith, but the spirit of Ethiopia still lives on - waiting to be revived by a worthy successor like us! ### Strategy[edit|edit source] #### Religion[edit|edit source] Three religious denominations exist inside Ethiopia at the start of the game:Coptic(the current state religion),SunniandJudaism. While a Jewish Ethiopia may offer an appealing challenge for experienced players, a Coptic or Sunni Ethiopia provides the best opportunity for success. ##### Coptic Ethiopia[edit|edit source] As a Coptic nation, Ethiopia can make early use of the unique holy sites mechanic thanks to the site in Aksum. The ability to give a boost to military (through manpower recovery (+10%) or discipline (+2.5%)), religion (through missionary strength (+1.5%)), or other aspects of the nation (through increased yearly legitimacy (+0.5) or reduced core creation cost (-10%) which is arguably one of the strongest points of the Coptic faith) based on the chosen holy site bonus allows Coptic Ethiopia to proliferate at a rapid pace, either through absorbing lesser countries or converting held provinces. Should nearbyMakuriaconvert the holy site in Qasr Ibrim from Sunni to Coptic, Ethiopia (as well as all other Coptic nations) will unlock a second Coptic bonus, further increasing Ethiopia's potential power. The conquest of Alexandria from theMamluks(which will be a short-term goal after domination over the horn of Africa is achieved, as to not let theOttomansseize control over the area, which will make the conquest of the holy site much more difficult) will unlock another Coptic blessing at your disposal. When conquering Egypt, it is best to take entire states in a single go. This is because there are a number of events that can grant +2 missionary strength for the whole state, for 10 years, upon taking a specific province in the state. Conversion is thus much easier getting these bonuses, and having it apply to the whole state and not be wasted. ##### Sunni Ethiopia[edit|edit source] As a Sunni nation, Ethiopia is less starved for

missionary strength for the whole state, for 10 years, upon taking a specific province in the state. Conversion is thus much easier getting these bonuses, and having it apply to the whole state and not be wasted. ##### Sunni Ethiopia[edit|edit source] As a Sunni nation, Ethiopia is less starved for decent allies in the early game. Muslim nations in the Arabia and Persia regions such asYemen,Hejaz,PersiaandQara Qoyunlu-- to say nothing of the mightyOttomans-- will find becoming friendly with another Sunni nation far more appealing than a Coptic one. As the game progresses, Sunni Ethiopia's expansion will be much smoother than its Coptic counterpart as it will not need to convert Sunni provinces taken through conquest. #### Diplomacy[edit|edit source] Starting as Coptic in a Sunni-dominated area of the world, religious differences can make finding good allies for Ethiopia difficult. Even if Ethiopia converts, there are stillFetishistnations in Africa that will reject alliances unless the player has grown to a formidable size. Regardless of state religion, Alodia and Makuria are prime targets for early alliances. Their positioning and size means they can provide decent military support while not blocking Ethiopian expansion to the north, east or south. There's even enough space to hand one or both nations a province or two from Beja or the Mamluks to keep the alliance strong. When seeking larger allies later in the game, the safest strategy is to look for matching religious interests. If Coptic, Christian nations that rival expansionist-leaning powers like Portugal,Spain,FranceandEnglandcan make for key assets during territorial wars. If Sunni, the Ottomans, Persia andMoroccoare excellent candidates, with Morocco having the added perk of also being eager to take Iberian territory from Portugal and/or Spain. #### Technology[edit|edit source] Ethiopia begins with the feudalisminstitutionand the 6/5/5 ruler Zara Yaqob. Nevertheless, your moment of technological parity with

assets during territorial wars. If Sunni, the Ottomans, Persia andMoroccoare excellent candidates, with Morocco having the added perk of also being eager to take Iberian territory from Portugal and/or Spain. #### Technology[edit|edit source] Ethiopia begins with the feudalisminstitutionand the 6/5/5 ruler Zara Yaqob. Nevertheless, your moment of technological parity with Europe and North Africa will quickly start to vanish once the Renaissance begins in 1450. ##### Speeding up institutions[edit|edit source] Though not an absolutely necessary strategy, forcing institutions to arise in your lands instead of waiting for them to spread from without provides an excellent return on the investment of monarch points: with a little luck and risk management, you will spend hundreds of monarch points on developing provinces to save thousands of points in increased technology costs in the future, and you won't have to constantly fight wars at a significant technological deficit. Note that Ethiopia's terrain poses an added challenge to this strategy, though. Without dramatic early territorial expansion, you'll only have rough and arid provinces with significant penalties to development costs to work with. On top of that, the low overall development of your provinces will greatly slow the rate of institution spread. The least costly provinces to develop within easy reach are two dryland provinces in your east and three dryland provinces initially underAlodia's control but often easily acquired. Arid drylands have a 20% development cost penalty, but you can completely offset it by fully unlockinginfrastructure ideasand making sure you adopt the encourage developmentstate edict. If you elect for this strategy, it's advisable to set your technology'snational focusto administrative technology: should Zara Yaqob live a reasonably long time or his successor have a respectable administration skill, you have a strong chance to finish infrastructure ideas long before the colonialism institution arises. To speed up institution spreading,

you elect for this strategy, it's advisable to set your technology'snational focusto administrative technology: should Zara Yaqob live a reasonably long time or his successor have a respectable administration skill, you have a strong chance to finish infrastructure ideas long before the colonialism institution arises. To speed up institution spreading, if and when you have some spare monarch points, you can spread them evenly developing a line of provinces -- which should include Gondar -- between the two dryland areas and then along a second line from Gondar south to Shewa. The two named provinces start as Ethiopia's most developed provinces and pick up new institutions relatively quickly. The thin smear of added development in the other provinces will make a difference in their adoption speeds and hence institution spread through the core of your territory.Alwaysremember to select the encourage development state edict before spending on development: the -10% savings may seem slight, but the monarch points you save will add up over time. It's also advisable as Ethiopia to force an institution to spawn as early as is feasible. You can only speed up the slow institutional spread in Ethiopia by so much, and thus an early spawn gives an institution more time to spread before the current technology penalty goes from annoying to onerous. With the Origins expansion there's another possible way to speed up the adoptations while lowering the cost of instititutions. The unique Ethiopian government reform gives a 75% discount to moving the capital. Once Ethiopia has gains some Europeans allies, these allies will probaly offer to share knowledge. By moving the capital just before the current capital state reaches 100% to another state the knowledge sharing agreement will stay active. Moving the capital is also beneficial for developing the costly to develop Ethiopian heartland by

has gains some Europeans allies, these allies will probaly offer to share knowledge. By moving the capital just before the current capital state reaches 100% to another state the knowledge sharing agreement will stay active. Moving the capital is also beneficial for developing the costly to develop Ethiopian heartland by pillaging enemy capital and concentrating development from vassal and unstated territories. ##### Idea groups[edit|edit source] Most of the idea groups can well benefit Ethiopia depending on your overall strategies. The below picks merely make use of Ethiopia's particular situation: As mentioned before, infrastructure ideas helps the institution spawning strategy when fully invested. It also has the potential to directly or indirectly boost income, though, this may be tougher to justify if not playing particularly tall. Economic ideas is also a viable alternative as Ethiopia to increase income, but this approach has more benefit associated to it when conquering through Egypt due to the income from economic ideas’ good produced modifier. If however you expand through Eastern Africa, trade ideas can greatly outstrip income from economic ideas, it's much more the long term investment since it requires Ethiopia to conquer many provinces in several different trade areas before it can overtake economy ideas' income potential. Innovative ideas can't fully offset technology cost penalties if you choose to let institutions spread to Ethiopia passively. Nevertheless, it's probably the easiest way to improve your monarch point economy, whichwilltake a beating from lagging technology and/or spawning institutions. Whether going with Sunni or Coptic, Ethiopia can convert other provinces reasonably well without picking up religious ideas. However, the added ease from religious ideas, besides its other nice bonuses, means quicker conversions with fewer frills. Since heretic provinces have a hefty production penalty, rapid conversions will also do much to turn around Ethiopia's economy. Furthermore, stranded

can convert other provinces reasonably well without picking up religious ideas. However, the added ease from religious ideas, besides its other nice bonuses, means quicker conversions with fewer frills. Since heretic provinces have a hefty production penalty, rapid conversions will also do much to turn around Ethiopia's economy. Furthermore, stranded in a sea of Islam and Fetishism, a Coptic Ethiopia can get enormous use out of the deus vult idea. Ethiopia begins with two vassals, can vassalize several neighbors in due time, and can even maneuver into an early personal union with its northern neighbor. Therefore, influence ideas could be a natural fit. Defensive ideas are always a popular pick to, ironically, improve your armies' offense. However, Ethiopia gets added defensive bang for its buck: the defensive mentality idea stacks its fort defensiveness bonus with Ethiopia's hostile borders national idea for +40% total. When placed among Ethiopia's copious highland and mountain terrains, forts therefore become hideously more difficult for even technologically ahead armies to deal with. And while attrition levels seldom cripple armies, defensive ideas eventually stacks an extra point of attrition on all enemy armies in your territory, most of which already imposes low supply limits, so the viability of delay and a scorched earth defense against a superior enemy significantly improves. Ethiopia does have viable options for a colonization game as explained below. If you have the urge to colonize the Indian Ocean islands and beyond, you'll need exploration ideas. Even if you have little desire to colonize in Africa, it's hard to pass up all the virtually free provinces for the taking once you expand beyond the Horn of Africa or towards the Sahara, in which case you'd likely want expansion ideas down the line. Keep in mind that it's hard to compete with EU4's big four

in Africa, it's hard to pass up all the virtually free provinces for the taking once you expand beyond the Horn of Africa or towards the Sahara, in which case you'd likely want expansion ideas down the line. Keep in mind that it's hard to compete with EU4's big four colonizing countries for lands beyond East Africa once their explorers reach the Indian and Pacific Oceans, so if you take exploration ideas, do so early (perhaps your second group pick) and probably avoid taking expansion ideas later since its colonizing advantages over exploration ideas will be shorter lived. #### Expansion[edit|edit source] Starting as an Empire, Ethiopia should try to unify the horn of Africa as its first objective. Its cultural union makes converting the land much easier, have low unrest, and gives a boost to tax income. Furthermore, the Gulf of Aden trade node is better than the Ethiopia trade node, so moving the capital/trade capital to there can also increase income. ##### To the north[edit|edit source] Making noise near the Ottoman Empire -- one of EU4's most dominating presences -- is a risky gambit at the best of times. To lessen the chance of fatal overextension, being on the Ottomans' good side either through an alliance or other arrangement is crucial. Should the Ottoman threat be temporarily pacified, theMamluksand all connected African territory beyond are ripe for the plundering -- especially if theOttomanshave decided to take a piece out of theMamluksfor themselves. ##### To the east[edit|edit source] The Arabian peninsula provides an alternate way into theMashriqregion that doesn't involve setting delicate plans in motion to deal with theMamluksandOttomans. Depending on who has allied with whom, taking territory off ofYemenand/orOmancan be done in a methodical fashion to avoid a hefty aggressive expansion penalty. (Note that the island province of Socotra

peninsula provides an alternate way into theMashriqregion that doesn't involve setting delicate plans in motion to deal with theMamluksandOttomans. Depending on who has allied with whom, taking territory off ofYemenand/orOmancan be done in a methodical fashion to avoid a hefty aggressive expansion penalty. (Note that the island province of Socotra is, in fact, Yemeni and may accidentally be overlooked by a hasty player.) From there, eastern expansion into Persia or northern expansion to secure all of Arabia awaits, assuming theOttomanshaven't already sealed off Arabia from Mashriq and are greedily eyeing expansion of their own. ##### To the south[edit|edit source] If colonists are not available, the Horn of Africa is the only way to expand Ethiopia south towards the Cape of Good Hope. This requires going through a handful of stubborn, low development nations, many of whom will not be so keen to see foreign infidels gobbling up territory in their vicinity. The amount of coastal land available on this route makes island-hopping a far easier prospect later on, not to mention providing a deterrent of sorts against European colonization. Be warned: even a rapid expansion may not be quick enough to prevent a European power from taking the Cape and all the trade node fortunes that come with it. ##### To the west[edit|edit source] If not colonized, the empty provinces to the west will birthFunjby event in the early 1500s. Assuming you or a vassal border it, you can start a war of total conquest on day one of Funj's existence. It begins its life with a very weak military and no allies, so it should be an easy conquest and well worth the -2 stability hit for declaring war without a casus belli. Annex every province: you probably won't acquire enough of an aggressive expansion penalty to matter, will

existence. It begins its life with a very weak military and no allies, so it should be an easy conquest and well worth the -2 stability hit for declaring war without a casus belli. Annex every province: you probably won't acquire enough of an aggressive expansion penalty to matter, will deny your rivals a convenient ally against you, and pick up a handful of provinces of Nubian (i.e. accepted) culture and Fetishist religion, which is easily converted to your preferred faith. From there, the path least traveled -- theSahelregion -- requires colonists to bridge the remaining gap from Ethiopia to central and western Africa. There is no more direct way toMali,Timbuktuand other African powers, many of whom will be more than a little unfriendly upon your arrival to their borders. If pan-African domination is the player's goal, securing this desert route is almost a necessary step. ##### To the sea[edit|edit source] An unorthodox method for Ethiopian expansion is overseascolonization. Initially, the closest feasible options are Comoros, Seychelles and Mauritius -- assuming the islands are free of European influence by the time Ethiopia is within colonial range. If at least one island can be added to the empire, the stage is set for Indonesian colonization. First, in order to get within the necessary colonial range, an occupied island will need to be conquered. Using Ethiopia's new holdings as staging points (Socotra also fits nicely for this operation), either theMaldivesorCeylonwill need to be captured. Of the two, the Maldives are less problematic as they are completely disconnected from the Indian mainland and less prone to retaliation. Once cored, Ethiopia should be able to make colonial landfall somewhere on the Indonesian archipelago. Once settled, expansion opportunities abound with the Philippines, Australia, the Pacific islands and potentially even the Americas ripe for colonization.

Once settled, expansion opportunities abound with the Philippines, Australia, the Pacific islands and potentially even the Americas ripe for colonization.

### Achievements[edit|edit source] Ethiopia has a specific achievement,Prester John, which requires control of a vast stretch of territory stretching from the mouth of the Nile to the Black Sea while remaining Coptic. As this means Ethiopia will eventually need to take land from the Ottomans, careful military planning will be needed to avoid disaster.

### Disasters[edit|edit source]

### Strategy[edit|edit source] #### Tuscany and Italy[edit|edit source] As an Italian minor, the early game is not easy, and it can be wise to wait for Europe's geopolitical situation to stabilize before setting rivals. Yet,LuccaorSienarepresent easy targets early on, unless they join thetrade leagueof eitherGenoaorVenice.Milanusually is the only available relatively strong ally at game start and can be a good ally against the powerful merchant republics on either side of the Italian boot. If Milan is rivaling the Emperor of theHRE(usuallyAustria), an alliance with the emperor might be preferable as it usually forestalls the "Unlawful Territory" demand. Milan also has its own expansionist agenda, so eventually (especially if the player aims to form Italy) one must be ready to break their alliance and confront them in war. WhenNaplesbecomes independent later on, it can be a great ally againstThe Papal State. Florence's mid-game goal is usually to formTuscanyand possiblyItaly. In order to form Tuscany, Florence must own its three starting provinces plus either Siena or Lucca. Vassalizing and then annexing them is not advisable as it requires more time. After Tuscany is formed, and theShadow Kingdom imperial incidenthas concluded, the player can expand to form Italy. Since Italian land gives a large amount of aggressive expansion from its high development, it can be a good idea to establish a foothold in North Africa, usually throughTunis, and expand there while European aggressive expansion cools down. Depending on the alliance situation after the Shadow Kingdom triggers, it can be good to ally nations that are rivals withAustriabut don't really want provinces in Italy, as it would allow the player to push north without competing with allies' interests.Franceis often the right choice; other options could includeCastile,Aragon, orHungary. Florence/Tuscany is in the unique position of being able to strike at all the provinces needed to

that are rivals withAustriabut don't really want provinces in Italy, as it would allow the player to push north without competing with allies' interests.Franceis often the right choice; other options could includeCastile,Aragon, orHungary. Florence/Tuscany is in the unique position of being able to strike at all the provinces needed to form Italy. With itsItalian Signoriagovernment, Florence is allowed to establish royal marriages with other Christian monarchies, as well as formingpersonal unions. #### Colonization[edit|edit source] The diplomatic strategy might require the early adoption ofDiplomatic,EspionageorInfluenceideas, which means sacrificing any real chance to colonization. But if chosen, the path of colonization can be chosen both early or late in the game. A good strategy could be to push through Egypt to gain access to the Red Sea and via that route to colonize Asia and Indochina, and push through to Australia. Alternatively, pushing through Morocco towards the rich gold provinces of West Africa is an optional strategy, although this route usually is more difficult because of the Reconquista by the Iberian monarchies. It could be good for the player to conquer parts of North Africa and get stronger with the help of colonies to then form Italy. If the player decides to do so, trade from Asia may be pulled from the Gulf of Aden trade node to Venice and from Timbuktu, trade may be pulled across the Sahara to Tunis and on to the Genoa trade node. Once Tuscany or Italy have become a great power later in the game, the player could choose to take over other nation's colonial nations to better expand its colonial domain. #### Development[edit|edit source] Florence is one of the strongest nations if a player is looking to play "tall" (i.e. stackingdevelopment costmodifiers to build up a small number of rich provinces). Florence starts as part of

could choose to take over other nation's colonial nations to better expand its colonial domain. #### Development[edit|edit source] Florence is one of the strongest nations if a player is looking to play "tall" (i.e. stackingdevelopment costmodifiers to build up a small number of rich provinces). Florence starts as part of the HRE and the Italian provinces mostly consist of farmlands. Florence has access to the Genoa end trade node and multiple centers of trade nearby. Another bonus is that the Renaissance is guaranteed to spawn in Italy. Gaining control over that province provides the Birthplace of Renaissance modifier, lowering development cost by an extra5%. Being a republic also means that the player can spend Republican tradition to reelect its ruler and generate a good amount of monarch points.

Traditions: The Taille Lessons of the Hundred Years war Estates General Native Trading Principles Divine Right Europe´s first standing Army Vauban Fortifications Ambition: However, the truce with England is tenuous. The English have still not relinquished their claims to the French throne, and one of the main demands of the new treaty,the surrender of Maineto France, has yet to materialize months later. While the Burgundians now passively support the French cause in the conflict with England, they are still a matter of concern for the French Crown. They have grown their domains far beyond the borders of France, into the Lowlands and theHoly Roman Empire. It is said that the Duke, who is also one of the richest men in Europe, harbors dreams of a future kingdom based around his collective domains. In 1444, the Kingdom of France is in a state of instability and flux, and French sovereignty is subject to the changing whims of their disloyal subjects. To our North are the English who wish to add the French Crown to their collection, and to our East is the rebellious House de Bourgogne who would dare to claim the throne for themselves. Before the French can even begin thinking about expanding their Empire, they must first ensure the loyalty of their subjects and integrate them into a centralized state, and remove the cursed English and Burgundian pretender Kingdoms from their border. France is free to pursue voyages of exploration, an active foreign policy inGermanyand theLow Countriesor even an attack on the rich cities ofNorthern Italy, where the imperial influence has been waning for centuries. France is free to pursue voyages of exploration, an active foreign policy inGermanyand theLow Countriesor even an attack on the rich cities ofNorthern Italy, where the imperial influence has been waning for centuries. Franceis akingdomlocated in WesternEurope, in the Franceregion, and could be one of the most (if not the most) interesting and entertaining nations to play in EUIV due to its powerful military and amount of flavor events. France allows for the player to make mistakes with usually limited consequences. Due to its military and sheer size, taking down France as an outside nation can be difficult, but not impossible. In1444, it bordersBurgundyto the north and east,Englandto the north and west,Aragonto the south,Savoyto the east and a large number of minor nations all around. Notable areBrittany,Provence, thePapal States, andNavarra. France starts out guaranteeing independence toScotland, in an alliance (as well as sharing dynasty) with Provence, and with cores on all the English land in France (excluding Calais). France begins with the unique "French Strong Duchies" nobility estate privilege, giving+3diplomatic relations instead of the usual+2– and needs it, as it begins with fiveappanages:Armagnac,Auvergne,Bourbonnais,Foix, andOrleans.

### Missions[edit|edit source] French missions are focused around uniting the decentralized French realm and further improving the economical power of the nation. The missions further the path of conquest of Italy through direct ownership of the land or through the use of the Golden March. France will also attempt to take control once again of theHoly Roman Empireto reclaim Charlemagne's inheritance. Dealing with England is also a major part of the mission tree and is fundamental if France is to rule the waves and become an important colonizer of the New and Old Worlds.

### Events[edit|edit source] #### Wars of Religion[edit|edit source] The rise ofProtestantism in Europe led to three decades of civil war within France known asFrench Wars of Religion. This disaster can occur in theAge of Reformation ifreligious unity is less than 75% and has Catholic, Protestant or Reformed provinces that aren't the state religion. Rival Catholic nations can form La Ligue Catholique, a major player in the French Wars of Religion, directed at the eradication of the Protestants in France. The League was used not only to defend the Catholic cause but also as a political tool in an attempt to usurp the French throne. The formation of La Ligue Catholique will decrease relations with Catholic nations and give qualifying neighboring powers a casus belli to restore the one true faith! #### Palace of Versailles[edit|edit source] Around 1650, the player will be presented with the choice, through an event, to begin construction of the Palace of Versailles. A high initial cost to set the construction in motion, as well as a temporary but lengthy tax cut, can be a very costly procedure, where several loans might be required. However, finishing the Palace of Versailles will give France the desired model of kingly ruling, and as long as the Palace remains, France will benefit from yearly prestige and legitimacy. With theLeviathan DLC, theVersailles great projectis upgraded toSignificant without paying the usual cost or waiting for the usual construction time. #### The French Revolution & Napoleon[edit|edit source] Thisdisastercan occur after 1710 either if the French economy is on its knees with a high number of loans, is suffering from highwar exhaustion, has highabsolutism, hasrevolutionary ideasspread widely or is the most powerful nation in Europe. This disaster can create revolts, uprisings and even the overthrow of the monarchy! Sometime along this disaster, the player

the French economy is on its knees with a high number of loans, is suffering from highwar exhaustion, has highabsolutism, hasrevolutionary ideasspread widely or is the most powerful nation in Europe. This disaster can create revolts, uprisings and even the overthrow of the monarchy! Sometime along this disaster, the player may get a 6/6/6 ruler named Napoléon Bonaparte.

### Strategy[edit|edit source] Note:Some guides recommend to look at other startdates to trigger bugs which are favorable for France (they lose their T1 government reform and become an empire). This is not recommended, because it also causes many other bugs. Most of these are not immediately apparent and some of them can ruin a campaign later on. #### Opening moves[edit|edit source] France starts out with 3 discounted +2 advisors : Jacques Cœur (+2 adm, -10% stab cost), Charles d'Orléans (+2 adm, -0,10 yearly inflation) and Jean Jouvenel des Ursins (+2 dip, +20% improve relations). Taking 2 of these 3 is recommended, as France starts with a very decent economy that will be strengthened when its cores in Normandie and Gascogne are reclaimed. Since you will conquer quite a bit of land in the early years, giving out your crownland early by granting all +1 monarch point privileges at the start is not a bad idea. Your first concern will be how to deal withEngland. They have fewer troops than France and are forced to navally invade, so defeating them in war and regaining the cores in Normandy and Aquitaine is not very difficult. France's thin coast means they start with a small navy and naval forcelimit, making naval invasion impossible. (This can be counteracted by getting military access throughScotlandbefore the war starts and invading England from the north.) The easiest option is to wait for theSurrender of Maineevent to fire. The two options for the English AI are to hand Maine back, triggering a short truce. More likely it will refuse to do so, instead launching a offensive war against you. This will make for a easy war, because as the defender, you will be able to call in any allies, while England won't. If Scotland is called in, England

Maine back, triggering a short truce. More likely it will refuse to do so, instead launching a offensive war against you. This will make for a easy war, because as the defender, you will be able to call in any allies, while England won't. If Scotland is called in, England will likely siege them down and ruin your war score. A quicker alternative is to declare an offensive war before the Maine event fires, just a month after the start of the game. This will prevent you from calling in your allies, but with your armies and vassal swarm, the war is winnable against England and its usual allies ofPortugaland Irish minor(s). By taking the initiative in declaring the war, you make sure that the English AI does not pick the option to give you Maine in exchange for a 5-year-truce, which is not in your best interest as you want to get your cores back as soon as possible. By allyingBurgundyat the start (if it hasn't rivaled you), you might be able to call in Burgundy in the war by promising them land, but it will take much longer to get enough warscore to give Burgundy Calais, and if they have more participant score than that, they will lose trust. Often they will declare a separate war, which works out better. Preparatory moves before this are to move your navy to your Atlantic ports. This allows you to blockade the fort when the English navy is not nearby and attack unescorted transports, hindering naval invasions. However your navy can not win a straight battle against the British and Portuguese navy, so avoid open battle and take opportunities as they arise. To aid this, use your higher siege pip General on the fort in Labourd, and using the other general

attack unescorted transports, hindering naval invasions. However your navy can not win a straight battle against the British and Portuguese navy, so avoid open battle and take opportunities as they arise. To aid this, use your higher siege pip General on the fort in Labourd, and using the other general on the Normandy fort. Portugal will get access throughCastile, so send more troops to guard against their attack on the fort, and get your vassals to attach to a stack nearby to reinforce, not the Normandy stack. An alternate strategy might be to leave allies and vassals sieging England's French territories, while you send your armies in Scotland before the war to fight directly in Britain. This would allow you to earn greater warscore, and to take some British provinces from England, which will form your starting base for the next wars against the perfidious Albion. Once the French forts are sieged down, head to Portugal. After being sieged down they can be offered a separate peace for gold, or if you are willing to commit to a long siege, their African fort in Ceuta (to fulfill theage objectivePresent on Two Continents). Eventually as ticking warscore raises, and England likely being hit with rebellions via theLollard HeresyandWar of the Roses, you will have enough warscore to take back all your cores. Additional objectives such as England's Irish province of The Pale or Calais may also be taken, but they take longer. Taking only your cores back fulfills initial missions to further claims and won't generate a coalition. Taking the province of The Pale would allow you to quickly expand into the British Isles through Ireland and Scotland, whose remoteness from mainland Europe will reduce aggressive expansion on the main powers of the continent. Eventually, an invasion of England from the

further claims and won't generate a coalition. Taking the province of The Pale would allow you to quickly expand into the British Isles through Ireland and Scotland, whose remoteness from mainland Europe will reduce aggressive expansion on the main powers of the continent. Eventually, an invasion of England from the North might be a much more secure path compared to an hazardous naval invasion. Once you get your Norman cores back, you can fulfill the"Liberate Normandy"mission in yourmission treeto gain claims on allBrittanyfor your next round of expansion. They will usually get one or two allies ranging in effectiveness, from a powerful foe like Castile to nearby OPMs. Given their position away from other nations, they can be fully annexed without triggering a coalition. Through the"Provençal Question"mission in yourmission tree, you can deal withProvencediplomatically by building up enoughtrustwith your ally (send a diplomat to curry favors in order to trade them for trust). The mission reward will make Provence your junior partner in apersonal union. While this option is preferable in terms of coalition management (as this gain of highly developed provinces won't generate any aggressive expansion), the number of free diplomatic relation slots you have is limited at the start of the game due to your important vassal swarm. As you won't be able to annex a junior partner until 50 years have passed, make sure that you don't need this diplomatic slot for stronger, more potent alliances. If you choose conquest instead, you can take advantage of the fact that Provence gets often excommunicated by the Papacy, allowing for the use of the excommunication CB, which greatly reduces aggressive expansion. #### After England[edit|edit source] From there, France has five common ways to expand. First, intoItaly. The French mission tree provides permanent claims in Italy, starting with Provence and

fact that Provence gets often excommunicated by the Papacy, allowing for the use of the excommunication CB, which greatly reduces aggressive expansion. #### After England[edit|edit source] From there, France has five common ways to expand. First, intoItaly. The French mission tree provides permanent claims in Italy, starting with Provence and moving on toSavoy,MilanandGenoa. This is a rich region and probably has the highest development in the game. However, it is a tightly contested region, and moving into it can make powerful enemies very quickly, especially the Holy Roman Emperor (usuallyAustria) if the player expands there before theShadow Kingdom imperial incidenthas triggered (usually in the 1460s) or if some of the targets have chosen to remain in the Empire. By the time the incident fires, you should have annexed some of your appanages, freeing diplo slots for diplomatic vassalisation of Italian minors. This will make conquest of the rest easier. Another good way to procede is by getting the"Transfer Subject" abilityin theAge of Discovery. It can be used to make other countries' subjects the player's subjects quicker and cheaper. It can be used to gain Naples from Aragon, which is probably most important since it is bigger than any other subjects on the Italian peninsula. Combined with some early conquests in Savoy, France can position itself to dominate Italy early. Secondly, you can expand into theLow Countries. It is easier to expand into than Italy, as enemies in the region are usually very weak early (with the exception of Burgundy who shouldn't be taken on alone early), and there are usually few other great powers looking to expand into it that early. Like Italy, its provinces are rich and will help you control the trade node shared with England. An alternative route to conquering these provinces is to wait for theBurgundian

shouldn't be taken on alone early), and there are usually few other great powers looking to expand into it that early. Like Italy, its provinces are rich and will help you control the trade node shared with England. An alternative route to conquering these provinces is to wait for theBurgundian inheritanceto fire. To trigger this event, the player must have initiated a royal marriage with Burgundy. If Burgundy has rivaled you first, you can ask them to cancel their rivalry in a peace deal. Then, if you improve relations with them, they will accept an alliance and a royal marriage. Nonetheless, the player needs to be aware of theDutch revoltsthat happen between 1550 and 1650 with large and recurring rebel armies. This can be avoided by either changing the culture of conquered Dutch provinces to Francien before 1550 or waiting with conquering the territories until after 1650. Alternatively, you can move your capital to the Low Countries region, but that only works as long as theNetherlandsdon't form by revolting from another country. Moving your capital into the Low Countries has also the benefit of moving your trading port to the usually richer English Channel node. Thirdly, you can expand intoBritainand England. Though unorthodox, taking English forts instead of your cores in the Maine war is a viable strategy. Expanding this way lends you relatively good provinces with the benefit that these provinces will never be touched bydevastationonce the conquest is complete. Should you go this way, it is advised to take the forts of London, Northumbria, and Calais in the Maine war (take as many of your cores as you can of course, focusing on forts and provinces needed for missions). With this move, you have shortened all future wars with England by stripping them of their fortifications, as well

take the forts of London, Northumbria, and Calais in the Maine war (take as many of your cores as you can of course, focusing on forts and provinces needed for missions). With this move, you have shortened all future wars with England by stripping them of their fortifications, as well as making the English navy completely useless. From here, England can be mopped up fairly quickly (assuming they don't get powerful allies). When taking provinces, focus on taking the forts in Glamorgan and Norfolk as well as the trade centers of Gloucester and York. ReleasingNorthumberlandisn't a bad idea, as they have many cores that can be reconquered. Fourth, one can expand south, intoIberiaand thenAfrica. Historically France invaded Algeria, and it is an easy area to move into if you take the Baleares or Sardinia fromAragon, or Ceuta from Portugal during a war with England. There is usually very little competition for the area with other great powers (except Castile/Spain), and the countries in the area are usually very weak, especiallyTlemcen. However, the area is Muslim, making it harder to convert and worsening rebellions. The region also has weak development, meaning that the gains made from it are smaller than in Italy or the Low Countries, but it is easier to take and maintain. If you're doing this, Religious or Humanist ideas are a must early on (though Religious is recommended for long-term benefits of actually converting provinces). The player may find an attack ofAragonprofitable if theIberian Weddinghasn't fired (yet) with the help ofCastileto take the Pyrenees, the Balearics, Sardinia or Sicily (to gain an access to North Africa), as it is an important and easy land to conquer, but also to consolidate French control over the Genoa trade node. During the peace deal, forcing Aragon to releaseNaplesas a sovereign state

the help ofCastileto take the Pyrenees, the Balearics, Sardinia or Sicily (to gain an access to North Africa), as it is an important and easy land to conquer, but also to consolidate French control over the Genoa trade node. During the peace deal, forcing Aragon to releaseNaplesas a sovereign state or even transfer it to France if possible will weaken both Aragon andSpainin future if it forms, and make conquest in the region easier in the future. It is equally possible to use the Balearics to releaseCataloniaand use its cores on the Spanish Mediterranean coast to greatly mitigate aggressive expansion in the area. However, trying to get apersonal unionon Castile or Spain through royal marriage might be preferable than antagonizing them, as it will allow you to get for yourself all their colonies in the New World once you have integrated them. The last path is colonialism. England, Spain, and Portugal will have better colonial expansion benefits through better settler increase, more colonists through national ideas, better control over the relevant trade nodes, and different colonial events. However, France's fourth national idea grants a−50%bonus tonative uprising chance, which stacks perfectly with the same bonus from theNative Trading Policy. It will give France no native attacks and a very good assimilation rate (giving a bonus to goods produced). This makes France among the best colonial nations without the need for moving troops into the New World. France also has some unique historical colonialeventsandmissions. Be aware, however, that in multiplayer France will often have strong enemies in the mainland, and oftentimes expansion might not be a good choice for the French player. After this, the game is completely open to you. If you are doing an active colonial game, France's mighty military will allow you to obtain some achievements that another colonial

If you are doing an active colonial game, France's mighty military will allow you to obtain some achievements that another colonial power would get with difficulty, likeMaster of India,Trade Hegemon, orThe Rising Sun. If you stay in Europe you will likely concentrate on the Holy Roman Empire and Austria. You can expand everywhere, the possibilities are limitless. #### Religion and religious wars[edit|edit source] During the Reformation, France gets a specialdisaster, theFrench Wars of Religion. This disaster is similar to theReligious Turmoil disaster, but with different events and trigger conditions (see its article for details). Because this disaster has very high chances of happening, you may choose to stayCatholic or goProtestant (orReformed) and both options will have the same consequences. Keep in mind though that going Protestant will remove theTreaty of Tordesillasfrom your colonies, but will give better bonuses than the papacy bonuses like colonial growth, morale, discipline, or a reduced development cost. If you join theProtestant leagueyou can try todismantlethe Holy Roman Empire during the league war if the Emperor and all the Electors are involved. This will remove the+50%aggressive expansion penalty of the HRE, so coalitions will be less problematic, and you will be able to easily conquer the German minors now that no Emperor will defend them. #### Taking French culture territory[edit|edit source] Francecan increase its power by taking territories withinthe French culture group. Neighboring countries in other culture groups will gain 50% lessaggressive expansion impact. Besides France's starting vassals, initial countries withFrench culture territoryare: - Brittany - Burgundy - England - Liege - Lorraine - Nevers - Provence - The Papal State #### Quick Roman Empire[edit|edit source] France

territories withinthe French culture group. Neighboring countries in other culture groups will gain 50% lessaggressive expansion impact. Besides France's starting vassals, initial countries withFrench culture territoryare: - Brittany - Burgundy - England - Liege - Lorraine - Nevers - Provence - The Papal State #### Quick Roman Empire[edit|edit source] France is one of the strongest nations for a Quick Roman empire, although it's a bit more prone to fail to RNG than nations likeAragon. France can attackByzantiumwithout a Casus belli on 11/12/1444. If this strategy is picked, naval invasion should be handled with care, as France's initial 2 heavies + 8 cogs are a bit less strong than the Byzantine navy. It's advised to ask theOttomansfor military access, in this case. Most of the time, in 1.35, the Ottomans start at -19 relations or so, and are not hostile, so a few months of improving relations to get them to positive will do the trick. In the month before the war, you should ally many nations and not be afraid of going over your relation slots, as it will be very helpful in the war. Ideally, you want about 4 ofBohemia,Aragon,Castile,Poland,Hungary,Venice,Burgundy(it seems like Burgundy starts always either Hostile or Rival in 1.35, so it's very unlikely) and other decent options areThe Papal State,SavoyandFlorence. It's not recommended to allyMilan, as the event « The Milanese succession » gives a Restoration of Union casus belli for only 5 years and it's likely to fire (it fires if Filipo Maria Visconti dies and Milan chooses the Ambrosian Republic). If going for a quick Roman empire, picking Jean Jouvenel des Ursins (discounted +2 diplomat) and Jacques Cœur (discounted +2 artist) as advisors is a great option, as Cœur will reduce the cost of increasing stability back to 0 or 1 after the no-CB war,

Milan chooses the Ambrosian Republic). If going for a quick Roman empire, picking Jean Jouvenel des Ursins (discounted +2 diplomat) and Jacques Cœur (discounted +2 artist) as advisors is a great option, as Cœur will reduce the cost of increasing stability back to 0 or 1 after the no-CB war, and des Ursins will make the aggressive expansion decay faster. Some care must be taken to make sure that the Ottomans actually declare war on Byzantium, but that the Ottomans don't get too many occupation to prevent the player from vassalizing Byzantium. After vassalizing Byzantium and taking Achaïe, Corinthe and Athènes (it's impossible to take Athens alone, as it's barely outside of coring range, and vassalizing Athens is pointless), the player will be dragged into a defensive war, where all the Appanages and allies will be able to participate. The player should keep an eye on theOttomansmilitary technology, as having a 6/4/6 starting ruler means that they are very likely to unlock mil tech 4 before you and your allies. In this case, just retreat, dance around their armies, and occupy their provinces when their armies are not there to impoverish them. When tech 4 is unlocked, you can engage in battles again. Whenever Burgundy flips friendly, if the Burgundian inheritance has not fired, ally them immediately, then send them a Royal Marriage offer. This will make you eligible for the Burgundian Inheritance, giving a lot of land for free. To avoid the Dutch revolts, it's possible to move the capital to a province like Bruges, which is in the Low Countries but not in the HRE. An event that grants a Subjugation casus belli onNaplesfires very often, and it's recommended to subjugate, as it will incur a lot less aggressive expansion than conquering directly, and Naples is a pretty

capital to a province like Bruges, which is in the Low Countries but not in the HRE. An event that grants a Subjugation casus belli onNaplesfires very often, and it's recommended to subjugate, as it will incur a lot less aggressive expansion than conquering directly, and Naples is a pretty useful vassal. #### Recommended idea groups[edit|edit source] - EitherDiplomaticorEspionage: This choice will allow better relations with neighbors and to avoid coalitions, which is critical when dealing with Western Europe high development provinces or the HRE. The reduced aggressive expansion via having extradiplomats improving relations and theimprove relations modifier, the reducedaggressive expansion impact or the reducedprovince warscore cost are very valuable. - Influence: As many opportunities arise through the game to gain junior partners inpersonal unions, Influence allow you to make good use of them and reduce theirannexation cost. - Exploration: You might find the need to colonize a little bit, since building the French army up to the maximum force limit and fully maintaining it provokes a mid-sized deficit without seeking additional sources of income. Colonizing the Caribbean and Gulf of Saint Laurence trade nodes will fix the economic problem (because these trade nodes are directly connected to the Bordeaux trade node). If you managed to conquer the British Islands and inherited or conquered the Dutch lands belonging to Burgundy, you may colonize the Chesapeake trade node, connected to the English Channel trade node, and collect the trade there. Colonizing Africa for later access to Indian trade can also be lucrative. - Expansion: This allows faster colonization if you're playing an active colonial game, and can thus help to make more money from tariffs or trade companies if theWealth of NationsDLC is enabled. - Administrative: This is an essential idea group for a world conquest because of the extragoverning capacity

lucrative. - Expansion: This allows faster colonization if you're playing an active colonial game, and can thus help to make more money from tariffs or trade companies if theWealth of NationsDLC is enabled. - Administrative: This is an essential idea group for a world conquest because of the extragoverning capacity and reducedcore creation cost. - EitherOffensiveorDefensive: Offensive provides a very strong +20%siege ability, and the general pips combined with high army tradition makes it very likely to get 3-star generals. In the late game, adding Force limit is always useful, too. Defensive, on the other side, provides cheaper armies and defensive, combined with the second national ideaÉlan!, will provide a huge bonus of +35%morale of armies. Thus, France is perhaps the easiest nation to get the "My armies are invincible!"achievement. Defensive doesn't shorten sieges, unlike Offensive, but it reduces losses due to attrition, letting you fight wars for longer. - Quality: Quality is far inferior to other military groups by itself, but it provides very interesting policies. It should not be taken if the goal is purely to win battles, as the best value in this case would be Defensive ideas. The best policy among these with Quality is Quality-Influence, giving another -10% diplomatic annexation cost, on top of Influence ideas giving -25%, and the Vassal integration act (Influence-Administrative) giving another -15%. This combines well with either the Crusader Kings path missions, giving France avery powerful vassalinThe Papal States, or with integrating a Personal unions obtained either through missions or royal mariages. - Quantity: More troops are always useful. This combined with Quality or Defensive will make the French army almost unstoppable. This can also be very helpful if you are facing a coalition war and have to send troops to all fronts, and of course, more troops mean more

royal mariages. - Quantity: More troops are always useful. This combined with Quality or Defensive will make the French army almost unstoppable. This can also be very helpful if you are facing a coalition war and have to send troops to all fronts, and of course, more troops mean more units to garrison the New World and Asian colonies. Keep in mind that this is completely optional, and this can change depending on your game. Example: If you're working on conquering Muslims in North Africa, it might be more useful to take religious or humanist ideas instead of exploration or quantity. #### Optional early moves[edit|edit source] - You can try to vassalizeNavarraearly on. An improve relations advisor and diplomatic interactions may help here. Great power influence, guarantees, allowing mil access, and gifts can all make sure you get the vassalization before they fall into the Aragon PU by event. - Focus on yourmissiontargets. Permanent claims reduce a lot of admin for coring and diplo (since you avoid unjustified demands), meaning you have more to stay on time in tech or expand further. - Going into Iberia generates less aggressive expansion. You can rush into the Berbers which generate almost no AE for Catholics. Make sure to try to get the Tafilalt gold mine in Morocco and state it for maximum income. The La Mancha gold mine is also a serious target. Devving gold mines to about 8 or so can really help with supplementing the already large French income. - The best way to get into Britain is to take one province in Ireland from the peace deal and attack from there with almost all your armies in the next war. Alternatively, you can attempt to vassalize an Irish minor, such asDesmond, for easy access. Lastly, as long as you

The best way to get into Britain is to take one province in Ireland from the peace deal and attack from there with almost all your armies in the next war. Alternatively, you can attempt to vassalize an Irish minor, such asDesmond, for easy access. Lastly, as long as you call Scotland (which you can ally day 1) into war with England, none of your troops in their land will be in exile, letting you keep the majority of your army in Britain to fight English forces on their own soil. - Italy is abnormally rich. Taking the entire Genoa trade node and making it your centre of trade can generate a lot of income. Take care about aggressive expansion though. Postponing until the Shadow Kingdomimperial incidentfires is advised.
